message:
refactor(output): reorganize module structure and apply formatting

Refactor the output module for better organization and apply rustfmt
formatting to test assertions. The module now properly exports its
submodules and types in a more discoverable manner.

Changes to output/mod.rs:
- Add denial submodule declaration (was previously missing explicit pub mod)
- Export DenialBox type from denial submodule for external use
- Reorder module declarations: denial before theme (alphabetical)

Changes to output/denial.rs:
- Expand DenialBox::new() calls in tests to multi-line format:
  - test_denial_box_plain_render: separate command, span, rule, severity
  - test_denial_box_with_explanation: same expansion
  - test_denial_box_with_alternatives: same expansion with method chain
  - test_denial_box_unicode_render: multi-line construction
  - test_denial_box_ascii_render: multi-line construction
  - test_denial_box_no_border: multi-line construction
- Format wrap_text test string with line continuation
- Expand test_denial_box_all_severity_levels severity array to multi-line
- Format assertion messages to span multiple lines for clarity

Changes to output/theme.rs:
- Expand Severity::from_str_loose assertion to multi-line format:
  - assert_eq! now uses separate lines for expected value

These changes improve code readability, especially for tests where
multi-parameter constructors are easier to understand when each
argument is on its own line. The module reorganization ensures the
DenialBox type is properly accessible to other crates.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
